plan.visual_reading: The arc opens on the empty tomb at dawn — 'it doesn't end in a grave' — then turns to the psalm scroll moving from the dark cross-lines to the bright praise of verse 22. The proof movement reveals the risen Christ alive, the One whose voice the praise-line becomes, and the Hebrews citation that names Him. The conviction is the risen Christ unashamed to call us brethren — His wounded hand on a brother's shoulder, the gathered family. The landing is the risen Christ with arms open, welcoming the listener in. The hero close is the welcoming risen Christ — the gospel-pivot of this resurrection short.
plan.red_team_notes: Jaded Viewer risk: a generic 'glowing risen Jesus' could read as kitsch — so the risen Christ keeps the marks of suffering (scarred hands), steady not floating, painted light not fantasy glow. Resurrection must be NT-warranted: the visual turn (cross -> empty tomb -> risen Christ among brethren) follows the Hebrews citation, not a bare-psalm over-read. Two scroll/David scenes (2,3,12) kept distinct. The hero is the WELCOMING risen Christ (gospel-pivot CTA), not the most triumphant pose. No bare-torso on the risen figure (robed, for clean animation).

scenes.title: The Empty Tomb
scenes.rationale: 'it doesn't end in a grave' — the empty tomb, not the cross
scenes.visible_elements: an open rock-hewn tomb, the great round stone rolled aside, folded linen within, dawn light entering
scenes.subject_block: an open rock-hewn tomb cut into a hillside, the great round stone rolled aside from the dark doorway, pale dawn light spilling across the threshold onto folded grave-linen left within, the garden in cool shadow beyond, no figure present
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of dawning, quiet hope after grief

scenes.title: The Psalm Turns
scenes.purpose: the turn from anguish to praise
scenes.rationale: the scroll moves from the dark cross-lines to the bright praise of v22
scenes.visible_elements: an open Psalm scroll, the lower lines in shadow and the v22 lines in light, faint vignettes of the turn
scenes.subject_block: an open Psalm scroll spread across pale stone, its upper lines of Hebrew sunk in cold shadow and its lower lines catching a warm break of light, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting a forsaken bowed figure, a turning toward light, a gathered congregation, and a standing praising figure
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of darkness lifting into praise
macro_elements: the warm break of light on the parchment

scenes.title: I Will Declare Thy Name
scenes.visible_elements: extreme close on a Hebrew scroll line, a fingertip beneath it
scenes.subject_block: an extreme close view of an open parchment, a single weathered fingertip at rest beneath one warmly lit line of ancient Hebrew script, the ink old and cracked, the parchment fibrous and lamplit, deep shadow at the edges

scenes.title: The Risen Christ
scenes.rationale: the risen Christ alive, the One whose voice the praise becomes
scenes.visible_elements: the risen Christ standing alive, robed, scarred hands, dawn light
scenes.subject_block: the risen Christ standing alive from a low angle, robed in a clean white-and-deep-red garment, his bearded face calm and living, his open hands turned to show the healed scars of the nails, soft dawn light around him, the dark of the tomb receding behind, painted light only

scenes.title: Among the Brethren
scenes.purpose: declaring the name among His own
scenes.rationale: the v22 praise in the midst of the congregation
scenes.visible_elements: the risen Christ among a gathered group, declaring, vignettes of the gathering
scenes.subject_block: the risen Christ stands robed at the centre of a close-gathered group of men and women, his scarred hand lifted as he speaks the Father's name among them, their faces turned to him in dawning joy, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting the open scarred hands, a gathered congregation, lifted faces in praise, and the cross now far behind
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a joyful, gathered family
macro_elements: a face turned in dawning joy

scenes.title: The Living Face
scenes.rationale: the marks of suffering past, alive and at peace
scenes.visible_elements: the risen Christ's face, alive and calm
scenes.subject_block: a close reverent view of the risen Christ's face, the eyes calm and living, a faint scar at the brow where thorns had been, warm dawn light on the cheek against soft shadow, an expression of peace beyond suffering
macro_elements: the faint thorn-scar at the brow
warm dawn light on the cheek

scenes.title: Hebrews Names Him
scenes.rationale: the Hebrews letter taking up the psalm's line
scenes.visible_elements: an open Greek codex page, a fingertip beneath a line
scenes.subject_block: an extreme close view of an open early codex page of Greek letters, a single fingertip resting beneath one line, the ink dark on the pale vellum, a warm lamplight across the surface, deep shadow at the edges

scenes.title: Not Ashamed to Call Them Brethren
scenes.rationale: Heb 2:11 — not ashamed to call them brethren
scenes.visible_elements: the risen Christ embracing a brother, vignettes of welcome
scenes.subject_block: the risen Christ, robed, draws a kneeling man up into an embrace, his scarred hand on the man's shoulder, both faces close in tender recognition, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting the embrace, a wounded hand on a shoulder, a gathered welcoming family, and an open doorway of light
macro_elements: the scarred hand on the shoulder
the two faces close in recognition
vignettes: a wounded hand on a shoulder

scenes.title: The Wounded Hand on the Shoulder
scenes.rationale: the scarred hand claiming a brother
scenes.visible_elements: a scarred hand resting on a man's shoulder
scenes.subject_block: a close view of the risen Christ's scarred open hand resting on a weathered man's shoulder, the healed nail-mark visible in the palm, the rough cloth of the man's robe beneath, warm light on the joined figures against soft shadow
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of being claimed and welcomed
macro_elements: the healed nail-mark in the palm

scenes.title: Calling You In
scenes.rationale: the risen Christ with arms open, welcoming the listener into the family
scenes.visible_elements: the risen Christ, arms opening toward the viewer, scarred hands, dawn light
scenes.subject_block: the risen Christ stands robed in white and deep red, his arms opening toward the viewer in welcome, the healed scars open in his hands, a soft dawn light widening behind him, his living face warm and inviting, the gathered family soft in the shadow beside him
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of an open, living welcome into the family

scenes.title: The Invitation
scenes.rationale: the risen Christ's face turned toward the viewer
scenes.visible_elements: the risen Christ's face turned toward the viewer
scenes.subject_block: a close reverent view of the risen Christ's living face turned gently toward the viewer, the eyes warm and inviting, a faint scar at the brow, soft dawn light on the face against gentle shadow
macro_elements: soft dawn light on the cheek

scenes.title: A Thousand Years Apart
scenes.rationale: David writing the praise-line and the risen Christ praising across a thousand years
scenes.visible_elements: David at his scroll at left, the risen Christ among brethren at right
scenes.subject_block: a wide two-sided composition split by a band of light; at the left, aged David seated at a lamp over a scroll, his face lifting in dawning hope; at the right, across the divide, the risen Christ stands robed among a gathered family in dawn light, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting the praise-line of Hebrew, a lifting face, the gathered brethren, and the risen Christ
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of wonder fulfilled across a thousand years
macro_elements: the risen Christ among the family

scenes.title: Welcomed into the Family
scenes.purpose: the listener's place in the family
scenes.rationale: a newcomer welcomed into the gathered brethren
scenes.visible_elements: a gathered group drawing a newcomer into their midst
scenes.subject_block: a close-gathered group of men and women in warm dawn light turning to draw a newcomer into their midst, hands reaching to welcome, faces open in joy, the risen Christ soft in the light beyond them, deep shadow at the edges

scenes.title: The Scarred Hands in Praise
scenes.purpose: the praise of the risen One, in one detail
scenes.rationale: the scarred hands lifted in praise — death turned to life
scenes.visible_elements: the risen Christ's scarred hands lifted
scenes.subject_block: a close view of the risen Christ's two open hands lifted in praise, the healed nail-marks clear in both palms, warm dawn light across them against soft shadow, the clean white sleeve falling back from the wrists
macro_elements: the healed nail-marks in the palms
warm dawn light on the hands

candidates.visible_elements: the empty tomb, stone rolled away, dawn light

candidates.title: The Risen Christ
candidates.visible_elements: the risen Christ standing, robed, scarred hands

candidates.title: Not Ashamed to Call Them Brethren
candidates.visible_elements: the risen Christ embracing a brother

plan.rationale: Opened on the empty tomb (the resurrection hook), not the cross; kept the risen Christ marked with healed scars and lit by painted dawn light, not fantasy glow; grounded the resurrection turn in the Hebrews citation (scene 7) rather than a bare-psalm over-read; hero is the WELCOMING risen Christ (10), the gospel-pivot CTA that brings the listener into the family, not the most triumphant pose.

panel.note: Opens on the empty tomb (resurrection hook), not the cross; coherent turn -> risen Christ -> family.
Resurrection grounded in the Hebrews citation (scene 7) not bare psalm; risen Christ keeps healed scars (Heb 2 brethren), no over-read.
State-only, Kling-cuttable; no banned tokens; risen Christ robed (clean animation) and lit by painted dawn, not fantasy glow.
Resurrection-variant Christ across many scenes — render with the resurrection ref for face consistency.
Hero #10 is the welcoming risen Christ (gospel-pivot CTA), correctly not the most triumphant pose.
Several risen-Christ scenes risk sameness — framings (wide tomb, low-angle, mid welcome, close face) and the family scenes differentiate them.

gates.evidence: Every beat id in ['hook', 'point', 'proof', 'conviction', 'landing'] has >=1 supporting scene.
the v22 turn + risen-Christ-among-brethren is fresh about the moment, orthodox in claim.
empty-tomb open + scarred risen Christ, not a generic glowing Jesus.
All subject_block + mood_block content clear of the banned-token list.
hero #10 risen Christ; lands on Christ.

State-only, no banned tokens, painted dawn not fantasy glow, robed risen figure.
Render resurrection-Christ scenes with the resurrection ref.

gates.evidence: Every beat id in ['hook', 'point', 'proof', 'conviction', 'landing'] has >=1 supporting scene.
All subject_block + mood_block content clear of the banned-token list.
hero #10 risen Christ; lands on Christ.

paper_cohesion.notes: The set forms a coherent visual spine on the resurrection-turn thread: it opens on the empty tomb (1), shows the psalm turning from shadow to praise (2,3), reveals the risen Christ and the Hebrews citation that names Him (4,5,6,7), holds the unashamed welcome into family (8,9), and lands on the welcoming risen Christ calling the listener in (10,11,12,13,14). No theology drift: the resurrection is carried by the Hebrews citation (scene 7) and the structural psalm turn, not over-read from bare Psalm 22; the risen Christ keeps the healed scars (consistent with Heb 2's brethren who share His suffering-then-glory), and no scene severs the Trinity or claims universal salvation (the welcome is into the family of those who come to Him). Every narration beat is carried by a strong scene; hook->climax->close (empty tomb -> risen Christ among brethren -> arms open in welcome) is a clean spine. Hero #10 is the gospel-pivot risen Christ, so the cut lands on the living Christ and His invitation.
